nodes.name: observability-readiness.js
nodes.summary: CLI gate script that performs a deterministic observability readiness check for ECC 2.0, inspecting repository structure for required hooks, configs, and doc markers, then emitting a scored report in text or JSON format.

nodes.name: buildChecks
nodes.summary: Core check engine that reads repository files and evaluates dozens of individual observability requirements, returning a list of pass/fail check objects with evidence and point values.

nodes.name: buildReport
nodes.summary: Assembles the full readiness report from check results, computing scores, identifying top failing checks, and structuring the output for text or JSON rendering.

nodes.name: renderText
nodes.summary: Formats the observability readiness report into a human-readable multi-line text string.

nodes.name: preview-pack-smoke.js
nodes.summary: Deterministic smoke gate CLI for the ECC 2.0 rc.1 preview pack, verifying required artifacts, manifest entries, verification commands, Hermes boundary markers, and absence of forbidden content.

nodes.name: buildReport
nodes.summary: Runs all smoke checks against the preview pack—artifact presence, manifest completeness, verification commands, Hermes markers, and forbidden content—and assembles a signed report with a SHA-256 fingerprint.

nodes.name: findForbiddenContent
nodes.summary: Scans a set of relative file paths for forbidden content patterns, returning a list of offenders with file path and line number.

nodes.name: release-approval-gate.js
nodes.summary: Final approval gate CLI for ECC 2.0 rc.1 publication, checking decision register approval states, URL surface coverage, announcement content rules, and approval-command presence before allowing outbound release actions.

nodes.name: buildReport
nodes.summary: Orchestrates all approval checks—decision register validation, URL surface coverage, announcement content, command presence—and returns a signed, fingerprinted report.

nodes.name: parseDecisionRegister
nodes.summary: Parses a Markdown decision register table into a Map of decision labels to their approval states.

nodes.name: findAnnouncementOffenders
nodes.summary: Scans announcement files for forbidden patterns—undrafted placeholder text, stale URLs, and premature links—returning offenders with file path and line number.

nodes.name: topActionsForChecks
nodes.summary: Derives the highest-priority remediation actions from a list of failing checks, mapping specific failed check IDs to concrete fix instructions.

nodes.name: release-video-suite.js
nodes.summary: Large release-pipeline CLI that validates the ECC 2.0 video production lane—verifying source media assets, suite render artifacts, publish candidates, and ffprobe-measured media properties—without embedding raw file paths in the output.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses spawnSync to invoke ffprobe for media metadata probing; the --skip-probe flag makes it usable in fixture/dry-run mode.

nodes.name: buildReport
nodes.summary: Assembles the full video suite report by inspecting source assets, suite artifacts, and publish candidates, running all validation checks and generating top-action recommendations.

nodes.name: probeMedia
nodes.summary: Invokes ffprobe via spawnSync to extract duration, video stream count, audio stream count, and file size from a media file, returning a structured media info object.

nodes.name: detectBlackSegments
nodes.summary: Uses ffprobe's blackdetect filter to identify black-frame segments in a video file, returning segment data for quality validation.

nodes.name: validateVideoArtifact
nodes.summary: Validates a single video artifact against expected duration, resolution, audio, and file-size constraints, collecting failure messages for each violated constraint.

nodes.name: evaluatePrimaryRender
nodes.summary: Evaluates the primary render artifact for duration, resolution, audio, bitrate, and frame-rate compliance, generating a pass/fail summary.

nodes.name: skill-create-output.js
nodes.summary: Provides the SkillCreateOutput class and supporting terminal UI helpers (boxed layouts, progress bars, animation) used to render the /skill-create command's analysis results to the terminal.

nodes.name: SkillCreateOutput
nodes.summary: Terminal output manager for the skill-create command, providing methods to render each phase of analysis (header, analyze, results, patterns, instincts, output, next steps, footer) using chalk-colored box layouts.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for WCAG 2.2 accessibility implementation, covering a five-step workflow (component role, perceivable, operable, understandable, robust) with web, iOS, and Android code examples and a best-practices checklist.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for auditing multi-agent architectures across a 12-layer stack, covering five common failure patterns (wrapper regression, memory contamination, tool discipline, transport corruption, hidden layers) with a four-phase audit workflow and severity model.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for running structured agent evaluations using YAML task definitions, git worktree isolation, and three judge types (code-based, pattern-based, LLM-as-judge) to compare agent outputs.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Concise skill definition covering the construction of Claude Code agent harnesses, defining core components (hooks, rules, commands, skills) and guiding when to build vs. reuse existing harnesses.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for debugging agent behavior by inspecting internal state, tool call patterns, context usage, and response quality, with structured diagnostic steps and root-cause taxonomy.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for integrating HTTP 402 x402 payment protocol into agentic workflows, covering payment flow, wallet configuration, error handling, and provider-specific implementation patterns.

nodes.name: high-score-example.md
nodes.summary: Worked example of an agent self-evaluation report achieving a high score, demonstrating expected output format and quality criteria across all scoring axes.

nodes.name: low-score-example.md
nodes.summary: Worked example of an agent self-evaluation report achieving a low score, illustrating common failure modes and deficient output patterns across evaluation axes.

nodes.name: evaluation-criteria.md
nodes.summary: Reference document defining the scoring rubric for agent self-evaluation, detailing criteria and weight for each evaluation axis (accuracy, completeness, clarity, actionability, conciseness).

nodes.name: hook-integration.md
nodes.summary: Reference document explaining how to wire the agent-self-evaluation skill into Claude Code hooks so evaluation runs automatically at session stop.

nodes.name: evaluate.py
nodes.summary: Python evaluation engine that scores agent responses across five axes (accuracy, completeness, clarity, actionability, conciseness) and formats a structured evaluation report with per-axis scores, strengths, weaknesses, and improvement suggestions.

nodes.name: check_accuracy
nodes.summary: Evaluates factual accuracy of an agent response against a reference answer by checking key claim coverage and penalizing contradictions.

nodes.name: check_completeness
nodes.summary: Scores completeness of a response by measuring coverage of required sections, code examples, and expected topics from the task definition.

nodes.name: check_clarity
nodes.summary: Assesses clarity by checking for jargon overuse, missing summary sentences, and readability indicators, returning a scored result with findings.

nodes.name: check_actionability
nodes.summary: Evaluates whether a response provides concrete next steps, commands, and examples that a user could act on immediately.

nodes.name: check_conciseness
nodes.summary: Measures response length relative to task complexity and detects repetitive or padding content, scoring conciseness accordingly.

nodes.name: evaluate
nodes.summary: Top-level evaluation orchestrator that runs all five axis checks and aggregates their scores into a final evaluation result.

nodes.name: format_report
nodes.summary: Renders the evaluation results as a formatted Markdown report including overall score, per-axis breakdown, strengths, weaknesses, and recommended improvements.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for agent self-evaluation, describing the five-axis scoring framework (accuracy, completeness, clarity, actionability, conciseness), workflow steps, hook integration pattern, and usage of the bundled evaluate.py script.

nodes.name: evaluation-report.md
nodes.summary: Template for agent self-evaluation reports, providing the standard structure for overall score, per-axis breakdown, strengths, weaknesses, and improvement suggestions.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for intelligently sorting and organizing agent outputs, task lists, or items by various criteria including priority, complexity, dependency order, and semantic similarity.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Brief skill definition introducing agentic engineering principles—decomposing tasks for multi-step autonomous execution, managing state across tool calls, and applying engineering discipline to agent-driven workflows.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive skill definition for the agentic OS paradigm, covering how Claude Code functions as an OS-layer orchestrator with 33 sections spanning process management, memory, inter-agent communication, scheduling, and security boundaries.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Concise skill definition for AI-first engineering, focusing on designing systems and codebases that maximize AI-assisted development velocity through clean interfaces, good documentation, and LLM-friendly structure.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for AI regression testing, providing a 19-section framework for detecting behavioral regressions in AI-generated outputs using snapshot tests, LLM-as-judge comparisons, and automated test harnesses.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for Android Clean Architecture, covering the three-layer structure (presentation/domain/data), Kotlin patterns, dependency injection with Hilt, and 23 sections of implementation guidance.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Skill definition for Angular development, covering component architecture, reactive patterns with RxJS, module organization, testing strategies, and Angular-specific coding conventions.
